title: Finance / Accounting Trainer (Freelance/Part Time)
description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Delivery of Core Accounting Concepts: Double Entry, Accounting Standards & Accounting Cycle •	Financial Statement Analysis: Guide learners on how to read and interpret the Profit & Loss (Statement of Comprehensive Income) and Balance Sheet (Statement of Financial Position). •	Interpretation of financials: Teach calculation of key financial ratios (Current Ratio, ROI, Gross Margin) to assess business health. •	Data Analytics & Visualization: Guide learners on extracting raw financial data and transforming it into actionable insights. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person, virtual, or hybrid). •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. Administrative Responsibilities: •	Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner. •	Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data. •	Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards. Learner Support and Engagement: •	Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve. •	Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment. •	Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students. Requirements •	ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required. •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. •	Professional certification (e.g., CA, ACCA, CPA) is a plus. •	At least 3–5 years of practical accounting experience. •	Strong presentation, facilitation, and interpersonal skills. •	Prior training or teaching experience is highly desirable. •	Ability to simplify complex financial concepts for diverse learners.

title: Financial Accountant

title: Assistant Accounting Manager (Tech MNC | Based in Vietnam).
description: Job Responsibilities Financial Reporting: Assisting in the timely and accurate preparation and review of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements, reports (P&L, balance sheets, cash flow), and general ledger activities. Supervision and Guidance: Overseeing, training, and mentoring accounting staff, reviewing their work (such as accounts payable/receivable and reconciliations) to ensure high-quality output. Process Improvement: Evaluating existing accounting practices and suggesting or implementing improvements to enhance efficiency and accuracy. Candidates with ﻿transfer pricing (TP) compliance and Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) will be an advantage. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Excellent organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks and tight deadlines. Willingness to take on overseas assignments and overseas postings. title: Accounts Executive (Full Set)
description: About the Role The incumbent will support daily full-set accounting operations and partner with the offshore Batam finance team to ensure accuracy, timeliness, and operational excellence in financial transactions. Reporting to the Assistant Finance Manager, this role focuses on hands-on accounting work, coordination, and follow-up to support month-end closing, reporting, and compliance requirements with the relevant stakeholders. Key Responsibilities Accounting Operations Handle daily accounting activities including posting/review of sales invoices, purchase invoices, payments, receipts, and journal entries. Perform reconciliations for bank accounts, e-commerce platforms, accounts receivable/payable, and intercompany balances. Support month-end and year-end closing activities to ensure accurate and timely reporting. Assist in the timely preparation and submission of GST returns in compliance with Singapore tax regulations. Maintain proper filing of accounting records and supporting documents. Work closely with the offshore Batam team to ensure daily accounting tasks are completed accurately and on time. Follow up on outstanding items, discrepancies, and documents with internal stakeholders (e.g. Sales, Operations, Procurement). Escalate unresolved issues to the Assistant Finance Manager when required. Reporting & Audit Support Assist in preparing financial reports and schedules for management review. Support internal and external audits by preparing documents and responding to queries. Follow established accounting procedures and suggest process improvements where applicable. Requirements Diploma in Accounting, Finance, LCCI, or a related field; candidates pursuing ACCA are welcome to apply. 1–2 years of relevant accounting experience; fresh graduates with relevant internship experience may be considered. Good understanding of accounting principles and Singapore tax regulations, including GST. Familiar with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central Accounting Software is an advantage. Proficient in Microsoft Excel, with hands-on experience using Formulas, PivotTables and lookup functions (e.g. VLOOKUP, XLOOKUP). Detail-oriented, organized, and able to follow up on tasks independently in a fast-paced environment. Good communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively with internal and external stakeholders. Positive learning attitude, proactiveness and accountability in assigned task.

title: Accounting & Tax Manager
description: Bestar is a full-service accounting and corporate services firm in Singapore. A Manager-level role at Bestar involves overseeing a portfolio of clients (ranging from SMEs to international subsidiaries) and leading a team of associates. Job Title: Accounting & Tax Manager Employment Type: Full-Time Job Purpose As an Accounting & Tax Manager at Bestar, you will be responsible for the high-level management of client portfolios, ensuring all financial reporting and taxation requirements are met with precision and in compliance with Singapore’s regulatory frameworks (SFRS and IRAS). You will act as a key advisor to clients and a mentor to junior staff. Key Responsibilities 1. Client Portfolio Management Oversee the preparation of full sets of accounts and management reports for a diverse range of clients. Review unaudited financial statements and ensure they comply with Singapore Financial Reporting Standards (SFRS) . Act as the primary point of contact for clients, providing advisory on complex accounting issues, business structuring, and internal controls. 2. Taxation & Compliance Lead the preparation and review of Corporate Income Tax (CIT) returns (Form C/C-S) and Estimated Chargeable Income (ECI) filings. Oversee GST reporting and ensure accurate quarterly submissions (Form 5). Provide tax planning and advisory services, including withholding tax, transfer pricing, and identifying relevant tax incentives. Liaise with the Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore (IRAS) regarding tax queries or audits. 3. Audit & Advisory Manage and coordinate year-end audit processes for clients, liaising with external auditors to resolve technical accounting issues. 4. Leadership & Operations Manage, mentor, and review the work of Accounts Associates and Senior Associates. Drive workflow efficiency through the adoption of cloud accounting software like Xero or QuickBooks Online . Monitor billing, collections, and the recoverability of team hours for assigned projects. Job Requirements Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Accountancy, Finance, or a related field. Professional Qualification: CA Singapore, ACCA, CPA, or equivalent is highly preferred. SCTP (Singapore Chartered Tax Professionals) accreditation is a significant advantage. Experience: Minimum 6–8 years of relevant experience in a professional accounting firm in Singapore, with at least 2 years in a supervisory or managerial capacity. Technical Skills: * Deep knowledge of Singapore Companies Act and Income Tax Act. Proficiency in cloud accounting software (Xero, QuickBooks) and MS Excel. Soft Skills: Strong leadership, excellent communication (English), and the ability to manage multiple deadlines in a fast-paced environment. Why Join Bestar? Diverse Exposure: Work with a wide range of industries, from local startups to international businesses. Professional Growth: Opportunity to move into specialized advisory or senior leadership roles. Collaborative Culture: Bestar emphasizes a supportive, team-oriented environment focused on SME success.

title: Senior Executive, Accounts

Accounts Payable and Accounts Receivable; Cash and Fixed Asset Management; and Ad-hoc tasks as and when required Financial Reporting & Month-End Closing: Prepare accurate and timely monthly financial closing, monthly balance sheet schedules and reconciliations, monthly cash flow forecasts and annual statutory financial statements Subledger Reconciliation & Resolution: Reconcile subledger transactions with the Financial Accounting System, perform investigation on discrepancies (if any), and resolve discrepancies to ensure accurate and reliable reporting Banking Transactions Management: Analysis Budget Preparation & Financial Forecasting: Assist in the preparation and consolidation of the annual budget and regular financial forecasts for assigned units Variance Analysis & Financial and variance analysis, will be an advantage Prior working experience in Social Services or Special Education sector will be an advantage.

title: Senior Accountant
description: Senior Accountant / Accountant (Retail & E-commerce | Singapore) Our client is a growing retail and e-commerce company with an established presence across physical stores, online platforms, and department store channels. As part of its continued expansion, the company is seeking a hands-on and experienced Senior Accountant / Accountant to oversee the full spectrum of finance operations in Singapore. This is a critical role that will work closely with business stakeholders and play an integral part in financial management, cashflow planning, and business performance. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated finance professional to take ownership of the finance function and contribute to business growth in a dynamic retail and supply chain environment. Key Responsibilities: • Lead and manage the full spectrum of accounting and finance operations for the Singapore business. • Oversee cashflow planning and cash management to ensure smooth operational funding. • Handle GST reporting, IRAS compliance, and corporate tax filings in accordance with local regulations. • Assist in the budgeting and forecasting processes to support strategic and operational planning. • Prepare financial reports, analysis, and insights to support management decision-making. • Manage relationships with banks, including banking facilities, financing arrangements, and all company bank accounts. • Develop, standardize, and strengthen finance policies, processes, and internal controls. • Supervise 1–2 direct reports within the finance team. • Partner with internal stakeholders across retail operations, supply chain, and e-commerce functions. • Support financial matters related to physical retail stores, online sales channels, and department store partnerships. Qualifications & Experience: • Bachelor’s degree in accounting, Finance, or a related field; professional certifications (ACCA/CPA/CA) are an advantage. • Strong experience in full-spectrum accounting and finance operations in a commercial environment. • Solid hands-on knowledge of cashflow management, banking matters, and financial controls. • Confident stakeholder manager, able to work closely with operational and business teams. • Hands-on, proactive, and adaptable, with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.

title: Accounts Assistant
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE: We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated
individual to join our Finance Department as an Accounts Assistant. Depending
on your strengths and experience, you may be assigned to either the Accounts
Payable team or the Bank Reconciliation team. Candidates who possess experience
in both areas will be highly preferred. In this role, you will support key financial
operations such as processing invoices and payments, reconciling transactions,
maintaining accurate records, and assisting in audit and month-end closing
activities. You will work closely with internal departments and external
partners to ensure the accuracy, timeliness, and compliance of financial data. This position is ideal for individuals with a
Diploma in Accountancy or a related field who are detail-oriented, organized,
and eager to build a solid foundation in accounting. You should be able to
manage multiple priorities effectively, work both independently and in a team,
and demonstrate strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting systems. ACCOUNTS ASSISTANT (ACCOUNTS PAYABLE TEAM) Key Responsibilities: Perform 3-way matching of supplier invoices
against Purchase Orders (POs) and Goods Receipt Notes (GRNs) to ensure accuracy
and compliance before payment processing. Process supplier invoices and payments
promptly according to the credit terms, ensuring that all supporting documents
are complete and in accordance with company policies. Communicate with suppliers and internal
stakeholders to resolve discrepancies or queries related to invoices or
payments. Manage filing and documentation of AP records
to support audit and compliance requirements. Assist in the preparation of accounts payable
schedules and month-end closing activities with GL accountant. Support annual external audit and periodic
internal reviews by providing relevant AP documentation and reconciliations. Perform other ad-hoc duties as assigned by the
AP Team Lead. Key Traits and Skills: Attention to detail and accuracy in data
processing and document review. Familiarity with 3-way matching concepts (PO,
GRN, and Invoice) in a high-volume environment. Strong Microsoft Excel and accounting software
proficiency. Working knowledge of Microsoft Business
Central 365 is highly preferred. ·Ability to work independently and as part of a
team in a fast-paced environment. Job Requirements: Able to start immediately or within short notice period will be an added advantage. Diploma in Accountancy / related- qualification. Fresh graduates and entry-level candidates are welcome, especially those with relevant or strong working experience gained during their school years. Good time management skills, ability to meet deadlines, and strong attention to detail. Good team player with the ability to work independently. Note: Our office will be shifted from Ang Mo Kio to Tampines in July 2026 / August 2026

title: Senior Finance Manager / Deputy Finance Controller
description: Role Overview The Senior Finance Manager / Deputy Finance Controller is a senior finance role responsible for overseeing end-to-end finance functions while supporting the CFO or Finance Controller in strategic, financing, and governance matters. The role covers full-spectrum financial management including accounting, reporting, budgeting, business finance, and capital management, with selected people management responsibilities. While the Group is not currently listed, the role requires experience in listed-company environments and a solid understanding of capital markets, governance, and reporting standards to support future readiness. Key Responsibilities 1. Financial Management & Reporting (Full-Scope) •	Oversee core finance functions including financial accounting, management reporting, budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis. •	Ensure timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reporting in compliance with applicable accounting standards and internal policies. •	Work closely with the Finance Controller on statutory reporting, audits, and financial close processes. 2. Business Finance & Performance Management •	Partner with business units and senior management to provide financial analysis, performance insights, and decision support. •	Support business planning, cost control, capital expenditure evaluation, and profitability analysis. •	Provide forward-looking analysis and scenario planning to support management decision-making. 3. Capital, Financing & Treasury Support •	Support and execute financing activities including bank facilities, refinancing, and funding arrangements. •	Prepare cash flow forecasts, funding analyses, and financial models to support liquidity and capital planning. •	Monitor banking facilities, covenant compliance, and treasury-related matters. •	Maintain working relationships with banks, financial institutions, and external advisors. 4. Financial Governance, Compliance & Listed-Company Readiness •	Ensure strong financial governance, internal controls, and compliance with regulatory and group requirements. •	Support audit processes, including coordination with external auditors and advisors. •	Apply listed-company standards and best practices in financial reporting, disclosures, internal controls, and governance, where relevant. •	Support preparation of board and senior management materials with appropriate governance standards. 5. Cross-Functional & Stakeholder Engagement •	Work closely with the CFO, Finance Controller, and internal teams to support finance operations and strategic initiatives. •	Support communication and relationship management with key external stakeholders, including but not limited to banks, shareholders, auditors, and government-related agencies, on finance-related matters. •	Coordinate and prepare financial information, analysis, and materials for stakeholder discussions and engagements. •	Contribute to continuous improvement of finance processes, controls, and reporting standards. •	Provide ad-hoc financial support and analysis for management requests and special projects as required. Key Requirements Professional Background •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline from a reputable local university is strongly preferred; a Master’s degree is an advantage. •	Professional qualification such as CPA, CA, ACCA, or equivalent is preferred. •	Prior experience in Big 4 accounting firms in Singapore is a strong advantage. •	Experience in listed companies is required, with exposure to financial reporting, governance, and capital market-related practices. •	Experience in China or PRC-related business environments is an advantage. Experience & Skills •	Strong hands-on experience across full finance functions, including accounting, reporting, budgeting, and business finance. •	Working knowledge of financing, treasury, and banking arrangements. •	Solid understanding of Singapore’s regulatory framework, corporate governance, and listed-company standards. •	Strong analytical, financial modelling, and presentation skills. •	Able to communicate effectively with senior management, auditors, banks, and external advisors. Personal Attributes •	Strong sense of ownership with a practical, hands-on approach. •	Able to balance operational detail with strategic perspective. •	High level of professionalism, discretion, and accountability. •	Comfortable working independently while supporting senior finance leadership. •	Fluent in English; proficiency in Mandarin is an advantage.

title: Finance Director
description: Shape strategy. Lead finance. Create meaningful impact. We are conducting a confidential executive search for an accomplished and forward-looking Finance Director. This is a rare opportunity for a senior finance professional to join a non-profit purpose-driven organisation with a strong track record of service excellence. The ideal candidate will bring experience in complex, multi-stakeholder settings and a passion for strategic financial leadership, operational excellence, and long-term sustainability. About the Role The Finance Director will provide strategic direction and oversight of the organisation’s financial functions for various entities, supporting a broad range of programmes and services for the community. This role ensures long-term financial sustainability, compliance with relevant regulations, and effective stewardship of resources. Responsibilities span financial planning, budgeting, reporting, funding, and audit activities, in close coordination with internal teams and external stakeholders including government organisations and statutory boards. Core Areas of Responsibility 1. Strategic Financial Leadership Develop and execute a financial roadmap aligned with the organisation’s mission and goals. Guide the executive/senior leadership and governance bodies on performance, planning, and financial risk. Perform scenario modelling and financial analysis to support strategic decisions. Offer expert financial input to key functional leaders and operations teams 2. Financial Planning and Budget Oversight Lead and coordinate the annual budgeting process across departments. Track and report on financial performance and recommending interventions as needed. Ensure delivery of timely and accurate financial reports throughout the fiscal year. 3. Revenue Optimisation and Funding Strategy Monitor financial indicators and streamline revenue processes for service units. Manage relationships and negotiations related to funding frameworks with external partners and Government organisations. Provide analysis and insight into funding model changes and long-term financial viability. Ensure compliance with grant requirements and manage billing and collection functions. 4. Governance, Compliance & Risk Uphold financial and statutory compliance in accordance with regulatory standards. Oversee external and internal audits and manage communications with auditors and authorities. Identify financial risks and implement proactive mitigation controls. 5. Procurement & Capital Resource Planning Supervise procurement strategy to achieve cost-effectiveness and control compliance. Lead planning and budgeting for infrastructure, equipment and technology investment. 6. People & Cross-Functional Leadership Build and mentor a capable finance team committed to continuous improvement. Collaborate across departments to ensure financial alignment with service delivery goals. Foster effective communication and integration across operational units. What We are Looking For 11 to 15 years of progressive senior finance and leadership experience. Current or prior expereince in a Community Healthcare or acute public Hospital environment involving multiple funding sources and regulatory oversight and compliance will be HIGHLY ADVANTAGEOUS. Demonstrated experience in managing Government ministry/statutory board subventions, including budgeting, utilization tracking, reporting, and compliance with funding requirements and audit standards. Solid experience in financial analysis, budgeting, grant management, and audit readiness. Strong leadership and communication skills with experience collaborating across functional areas. Comfortable navigating complex stakeholder environments. Proficient in both written and verbal communication. Preferred Background Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. CPA or equivalent professional certification preferred. Postgraduate qualifications such as an MBA or MHA will be an added advantage. The selected candidate can look forward to a competitive remuneration package consisting of basic salary, 13th month AWS and variable/performance bonuses. Interested and suitably qualified applicants, please apply here or email to:- jerry.loo@recruit-inc.com Reg No: R1550439 EA Licence No: 14C7334 We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

description:
This position is the Financial Operations Manager for the unit and is responsible for overseeing and
performing the full range of financial planning and budget formulation for the Mission. Requirements: All applicants must address each selection criterion detailed below with specific and comprehensive information supporting each item in their application.  Failure to do so may result in a determination that the applicant is not qualified. Work Experience and Education: Option 1 (Full Performance Grade): At least seven (7) years progressively responsible experience in one or more of the following areas: accounting, budgeting, financial management, vouchering, allowances, and cashiering required. At least one (1) year of supervisory or team lead experience is required. A bachelor's degree in finance, accounting or business administration is required. Capable of motivating subordinates and supervise impartially. Able to use fully MS Word, Outlook PowerPoint, and Advanced Excel skills and Outlook and Word. title: Finance Executive (Financial Operations and Digital Transformation)
description: About Us The Singapore Badminton Association (SBA), established in 1929, is the national governing body for badminton in Singapore. Our mission is to nurture world-class athletes, promote grassroots development, and elevate the standards of badminton through innovation and strategic partnerships. As we strive to make Singapore a globally recognised badminton powerhouse, we need a strong finance team to support this vision. Role Overview We are seeking a Finance Executive with a primary focus on Finance Ops activities with secondary responsibilities in supporting digitalisation initiatives. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a dynamic environment, values accuracy, and is eager to contribute to modernising financial processes. Key Responsibilities Finance Ops – AR, GL, Fixed Assets & Grants Management Ensure timely and accurate billing, invoicing, collections, and reconciliation. Monitor outstanding receivables and manage cash flow effectively. Liaise with internal and external stakeholders to resolve discrepancies promptly. Manage journal entries, accruals, adjustments, reclassifications, and monthly GL reconciliations (e.g., bank, intercompany, suspense/clearing accounts). Support the month-end and year-end close by preparing schedules, validating balances, and providing audit-ready documentation. Maintain the fixed asset register, including capitalisation, disposals, depreciation, transfers, and periodic physical verification. Prepare fixed asset reports for budgeting, forecasting, and audits while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and internal policies. Oversee grant financials, including budget tracking, disbursements, claims, utilisation, and compliance with funding agency requirements. Prepare and submit grant reports and reimbursement claims with complete supporting documents, resolving discrepancies with program teams and grantors. Financial Reporting & Internal Controls Maintain accurate financial records, documentation, and schedules to meet audit, regulatory, and internal compliance requirements. Identify gaps in processes, documentation, and internal controls, recommending improvements to the Finance Manager/Head of Finance. Prepare periodic financial reports, support month-end reporting activities, and ensure timely submission of reconciliations and compliance deliverables. Accounts Payable Support Assist during peak periods or when AP resources are unavailable. Digitalisation & Process Improvement Use Xero and Airwallex systems for efficient financial management and reporting. Work with the Finance Manager on projects to digitise QuotetoCash (Q2C) processes and donor management systems. Identify opportunities to automate reconciliations, dashboards, and workflows to improve accuracy and timeliness. Team Collaboration Work closely with cross-functional teams to support financial operations and continuous improvement initiatives. Reporting to: Finance Manager and Head of Finance Requirements 2–3 years of relevant experience (non-profit, startup, or SME experience preferred). Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (or equivalent experience). Proficiency in Xero and familiarity with the Airwallex ecosystem. Advanced Microsoft Excel skills and willingness to learn new systems. Strong attention to detail, ability to manage multiple priorities, and adapt to a fast-paced environment. What We Offer Competitive salary and benefits package. Opportunities for portfolio development in digital transformation projects. A collaborative work environment with a mission-driven team.

The Nanyang Executive Centre (NEC) is located at the NTU Main Campus. Currently undergoing refurbishment, the NEC features 150 guestrooms and aims to offer a premier venue for corporate training, retreats and private events. We are looking for an experienced person to join us as Assistant Manager, Finance. The Assistant Manager, Finance will be an integral part of the NEC Executive Office, playing a critical role in ensuring accurate management of NEC’s financial operations, including Cost Control, General Ledger maintenance, procurement and receiving processes. Ensure adherence to local and international financial and tax regulations. Identify and mitigate financial risks, develop contingency plans, and ensure compliance with regulations. Conduct half-yearly inventory count and reconciliation. Review contracts and invoices and ensure basic contractual compliance for outsourced partners/ vendors. Implement and monitor internal finance controls. Identify opportunities to improve processes and enhance NEC efficiency. title: Finance Manager
description: About the Role We are looking for a high-energy, hands-on Finance Manager to serve as a change agent within our growing company. This is not just a "keeping the lights on" role; you will be the architect of our financial infrastructure. Your mission is to evolve our finance department from a transactional back-office function into a streamlined, tech-enabled powerhouse that provides the strategic insights necessary for our next phase of growth. Key Responsibilities Process Engineering & Automation Conduct a comprehensive review of existing workflows (AP, AR, GL, Inventory) to identify bottlenecks and implement lean methodologies. Lead the transition from manual entry to automated workflows, leveraging ERP capabilities and third-party integrations to reduce "human-in-the-loop" errors. Design, document, and enforce robust SOPs and internal controls that ensure audit readiness without sacrificing operational speed. Financial Intelligence & Reporting Optimize the month-end closing cycle to deliver faster, more accurate financial reporting. Beyond standard P&L, develop dynamic financial dashboards focusing on unit economics, margin leakage, and working capital efficiency. Bridge the gap between raw numbers and business strategy by providing actionable variance analysis and trend forecasting to the leadership team. Strategic Partnership & Compliance Collaborate with Operations and Commercial teams to analyze product profitability and optimize cost structures. Proactively identify operational and financial risks, implementing practical controls that scale with the business. Overset all statutory obligations, including tax filings, audit coordination, and compliance with the latest accounting standards. Lead and upskill a lean finance team, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and professional excellence. Experience & Qualifications Degree in Accounting/Finance; professional qualification (CA, CPA, or ACCA) is highly preferred. 6–10 years of experience, with at least 3 years in a "lean team" environment where you were required to be both a strategist and a doer. Advanced proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite, SAP B1, Xero) and data visualization tools. You should be someone who "breaks" and then "fixes" spreadsheets to make them better. You are comfortable with ambiguity and enjoy the challenge of building processes from scratch rather than just following existing ones. You don’t just report numbers; you interrogate them until they tell a story. Ability to explain complex financial concepts to non-finance stakeholders with clarity and empathy. Job Highlights Annual Leave - 14 days onwards Birthday Leave - We believe employee should take a day off to celebrate their birthday with their loved ones. FREE TCM services and Tech-enhanced therapies for our employees, and 50% discount for employees’ family members! Attractive and Good Commissions Entitlement Great working culture - Fun and enjoyable workplace that embraces Diversity and Work-life balance/harmony. Why Should You Join Us? GREAT CULTURE AND ATMOSPHERE: Because you will be working along with amazing and crazy fun people. We have cyclists, chefs, yogis, gamers, athletes, animal lovers, motivational and inspirational trainers, foodies, and many more. You’ll definitely love your #workfam. GREAT SALARY PACKAGE AND BENEFITS: Because we offer benefits which are most desired and valued by people. We believe that the people in the team are the backbone of the company, the core of everything we do. This is why we invest so much in our employee’s welfare and future. GROWTH AND CAREER DEVELOPMENT: Because the sky's the limit and we firmly believe that our employees' growth, development, and well-being are the most significant factor. We hope to grow with our employees, we want our people to shine brighter as they progress with the company. DIVERSITY: Because we value equity and inclusion. We are an equal opportunity employer who makes decisions without regard to age, identity, gender, race, colour, ethnicity, etc. We encourage our employees to be their true selves at work.

title: Finance Manager
description: About the job Rently is looking for a Finance Manager to own the financial core of the business. We operate a fintech and lending business across Singapore and Dubai. As we scale, the financial complexity of the business is increasing, and we’re investing in stronger planning, visibility, and decision support. We’re looking for someone who deeply understands how money flows through the business and can help leadership make better decisions, faster. This is our first dedicated finance hire and a critical role at Rently. You will sit at the center of planning, performance tracking, and decision support. Your primary focus will be FP&A, financial modelling, unit economics, and cash visibility. You’ll work directly with the CEO and closely with the leadership team. You’ll be supported by internal accountants who handle day-to-day bookkeeping and a fractional CFO who acts as a senior advisor. Your role sits in between: hands-on, analytical, and close to the business. If you like building clarity from messy inputs and want real ownership in an early-stage fintech, this role is for you. What You’ll Do Primary Responsibilities (Day-to-day, must be excellent) 📐 Financial Modelling & Forecasting Build, own and maintain Rently’s core financial model used for planning, fundraising, and decision-making Own rolling forecasts, key assumptions, debt investor reporting and scenario analysis Update projections as the business evolves and flag issues early 📊 Unit Economics & Portfolio Analytics Own unit economics across products and markets Track yields, defaults, provisioning, contribution margins, cohort performance, PAR30, write-offs, and IRR Help leadership understand what is working, what is not, and why 💸 Burn, Runway & Cash Visibility Maintain a clear, up-to-date view of burn, runway, and short-term liquidity Support the CEO on capital deployment decisions and pacing of growth 🤝 Leadership Decision Support Partner with the CEO/Founders and leadership team on pricing, hiring pace, market expansion, and growth vs. profitability trade-offs Prepare clear, concise materials for leadership, board, and investor discussions Secondary Responsibilities (Support / Coordination) 🧾 Accounting & Data Reliability Partner closely with internal and external accountants to ensure clean, timely, and reliable financial data You won’t do day-to-day bookkeeping, but you are accountable for having accurate numbers to drive analysis and planning 📜 Regulatory, Investor & Debt Reporting (Numbers-focused) Support regulatory and compliance reporting in Singapore and the UAE by owning financial inputs (not filings) Support debt investor reporting using existing templates and guidance Provide financial inputs for audits, tax, and licensing processes 🌍 Multi-Market Financial View Consolidate Singapore and Dubai into a single financial view Oversee FX exposure, intercompany considerations, and market-level P&Ls (oversight, not structuring) 📊 Financial Data & Analysis Pull and analyze data directly where possible (without relying heavily on engineering) Build dashboards or analyses to track performance, defaults, and unit economics using available tools Who You Are ✅ FP&A-first, Hands-on Finance Operator ~7–10 years of experience in finance, FP&A, or related roles Strong financial modelling and forecasting skills Comfortable getting into the details and building models yourself ✅ Strong Foundation in Accounting Background in audit or accounting (Big 4 or in-house) strongly preferred You understand how financial statements are built and how to pressure-test numbers ✅ Experience with Capital-Intensive or Lending Models Experience in fintech, lending, BNPL, or other capital-intensive businesses is a strong plus Comfortable working with loan books, portfolios, or cohort-based economics ✅ Clear Thinker & Communicator Able to explain numbers clearly to non-finance teammates Focused on what matters, not just what is technically correct ✅ Independent & Practical Comfortable with ambiguity and imperfect systems Able to create structure, prioritize, and move without heavy direction ⭐ Bonus: Experience at an early-stage or high-growth startup Comfortable pulling data using SQL Experience with BI or analytics tools (Looker, Metabase, Tableau, Power BI) Experience working across multiple markets or entities Tools & System Strong Excel / Google Sheets modelling skills required Experience with accounting systems such as Xero (or similar) preferred Comfortable working with raw data exports and financial systems Growth Path This is a foundational finance hire. As Rently scales, this role has clear upside: Opportunity to grow into a Head of Finance role over time Opportunity to build and manage a small finance team Increasing influence over business strategy, capital planning, and investor relationships Why Join Rently 🏙️ Build a meaningful fintech business – Help shape the future of renting and payments across multiple markets. 🤝 Work closely with leadership – Partner directly with the CEO on the most important decisions in the business. 📈 Real ownership – This is a foundational hire with real responsibility and impact. 🌍 Flexibility & growth – Hybrid setup in Singapore with exposure to regional expansion as we scale. 📢 Note: Direct applicants only — we’re not working with agencies for this role.

description: What you'll do: As an Accounts Payable Specialist in this regional finance team based in Singapore, you will be entrusted with responsibilities that span supervision of payment operations, cashflow forecasting under sophisticated arrangements, policy enforcement for compliance purposes, vendor data management, issue resolution for stakeholders at all levels, identification of process inefficiencies for improvement recommendations, participation in business maturity initiatives leveraging technology or operational enhancements, contribution to a nurturing team culture through knowledge sharing and supportiveness, as well as upholding professionalism in every aspect of your work. Your day-to-day activities will involve close coordination with various departments including tax and treasury functions across APAC regions. Success in this role requires enthusiasm for learning new skills within a fast-evolving environment where your input directly influences operational excellence. Forecast and monitor cashflow across the region under a cash pooling arrangement while coordinating effectively with the Treasury team on liquidity management. Establish, document, and enforce policies, processes, and controls within the accounts payable function to ensure compliance with relevant standards and regulations. Maintain vendor data integrity by overseeing regular updates and ensuring adherence to statutory requirements. Act as the main point of contact for resolving complex issues or queries from both internal teams and external vendors, evaluating situations thoroughly before recommending process improvements. Identify inefficiencies within current accounts payable practices and recommend enhancements that promote greater efficiency or compliance outcomes. Participate actively in continuous business maturity projects alongside other members of the finance team as well as wider organisational units to implement operational improvements or technological solutions. Contribute positively to a collaborative team culture by sharing knowledge, supporting colleagues’ development, and fostering an inclusive environment. Ensure all activities are conducted with professionalism, discretion, and attention to detail while maintaining high standards of accuracy. What you bring: To excel as an Accounts Payable Specialist in this organisation’s regional finance team based in Singapore, you will bring proven expertise managing payables functions, ideally gained within multinational environments, and hands-on involvement in integration projects linking banking platforms with enterprise resource planning systems. Your comprehensive grasp of indirect tax rules across APAC markets will underpin your ability to maintain compliance while implementing robust financial controls. A genuine enthusiasm for acquiring new skills sets you apart as someone who embraces ongoing professional development. Your exceptional organisational capabilities ensure accuracy even when handling complex transactions or sensitive data. Effective communication, both written and verbal, is essential for building strong relationships internally as well as externally. You flourish amid flexibility and change thanks to your commitment to quality outcomes. Professionalism guides your interactions at all times while your willingness to share knowledge supports collective success within the finance team. Demonstrated experience managing accounts payable functions within a regional or multinational organisation; prior leadership experience is highly desirable. Proven track record supporting integration projects between banking platforms and ERP systems. Comprehensive understanding of indirect tax regulations and statutory reporting requirements relevant to APAC jurisdictions. Hands-on experience implementing financial processes and controls; familiarity with Oracle Fusion is preferred but not mandatory. Enthusiastic approach towards learning new skills coupled with a passion for personal development within finance operations. Exceptional organisational abilities complemented by meticulous attention to detail when handling sensitive financial information. Outstanding communication skills, both written and verbal, that enable effective collaboration across teams and external partners. Ability to thrive in an inclusive environment characterised by flexibility, dynamism, and rapid change without compromising quality or accuracy. High degree of professionalism combined with discretion when dealing with confidential matters or complex stakeholder queries. Commitment to fostering teamwork through knowledge sharing while supporting colleagues’ growth within a communal setting. What sets this company apart: This organisation stands out for its commitment to creating an inclusive workplace where every individual’s contributions are valued. Employees benefit from flexible working arrangements designed to accommodate diverse needs while promoting work-life balance. The company invests heavily in training opportunities so you can continue expanding your skillset throughout your career journey. Supportive leadership fosters an environment where collaboration thrives, whether you’re working on cross-functional projects or sharing insights during team meetings. Knowledge sharing is encouraged at every level so you always feel connected within a dependable network of professionals who care about each other’s growth. The company’s reputation for operational excellence is matched only by its dedication to nurturing talent through generous support structures that help you achieve both personal fulfilment and professional advancement. title: Product Costing Analyst
description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Product Costing Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will be a key player in managing our product costs and ensuring the financial health of our manufacturing operations. You will collaborate extensively with cross-functional teams to drive our quarterly costing process, analyze cost variances, and provide critical insights that directly impact our profitability. Key Responsibilities Quarterly Cost Review: Coordinate quarterly internal cost review exercise by collaborating with Global Supply Management, Program Management, Materials, and Finance teams. Ensure the timely collection and validation of data from all stakeholders to maintain accurate product costing. Variance Analysis & Forecasting: Develop detailed forecasts and track the plant's Purchase Price Variance (PPV), providing actionable insights into material cost fluctuations. Perform root cause analysis on cost variances, identify key drivers, and present findings and recommendations to management for corrective action. Cost Management & Recovery: Actively monitor the plant's cost recovery programs to ensure all eligible expenses are accurately tracked, documented, and reclaimed. Partner with the Finance team to validate and reconcile cost recovery claims, maximizing financial returns for the facility. Job Requirements Degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, Engineering or equivalent. 2 to 4 years of experience in Cost Accounting, Materials, Program Product Costing, or a similar role, preferably within a manufacturing environment. Proficiency in Microsoft Excel is essential (e.g., complex formulas, pivot tables, data modeling) and other MS Office applications. Hands-on experience with an ERP system (such as SAP, Oracle, or equivalent) is highly desirable. Strong data analysis and financial modeling capabilities. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with a proven ability to work effectively across multiple departments. A highly analytical mindset with strong problem-solving abilities and meticulous attention to detail. Exceptional organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities under tight deadlines.

title: Fresh Graduates - Bank Operations
best_module: ACC4713 – Banking Institutions:Managing Risks in a DigitalWorld
description: This is a back-office operations role in a bank’s Card Operations Processing Centre, specifically within the Reconciliation and Investigation Unit. Your main responsibility is to ensure that the bank’s financial records match actual money movement, particularly for credit card transactions. Responsibilities: - Daily reconciliation/proofing of General Ledger (GL) and Nostro (Cash accounts) on transactions for Cards Operations Processing Centre - Escalation of outstanding items for the day, and age items for both Nostro and General Ledger (GL) - Invoice reconciliation" (A) Key Duties Explained: - Daily Reconciliation of Accounts: You will compare and match bank records (General Ledger) with actual cash balances (Nostro accounts) to ensure there are no discrepancies. Think of this as daily “balancing the books” — making sure the bank’s internal system reflects the real-world financial activity. - Escalating Outstanding Items: If any mismatch or error is found (e.g., a payment didn’t go through or an amount doesn’t match), you will flag or escalate it to the relevant team for investigation and resolution. - Aging of Items: You'll monitor unresolved discrepancies over time. “Aging” refers to tracking how long items remain unmatched and ensuring they are not left unresolved for too long. - Invoice Reconciliation: You will help match invoices received (e.g., from vendors or third-party processors) against the bank’s records, ensuring correctness before payment or settlement. (B) Skills You’ll Use: - Attention to detail (to spot mismatches), Familiarity with Excel or reconciliation tools, Ability to follow up and escalate when issues arise, Time management (to complete daily checks promptly) - Who is This Job Suitable For: Fresh graduates in banking, finance, or business, or Candidates with 1–2 years of back-office banking experience, Someone who prefers structured, process-oriented work rather than customer-facing roles

title: Finance Executive (AR)
description: Job Summary We are looking for a detail-oriented and proactive Accounts Receivable Executive to join our Finance team. In this role, you will manage core AR functions including daily sales reconciliation across multiple platforms, GTO calculation, voucher reimbursement processing, invoicing, receivable tracking, and collections. You will work closely with outlet teams, customers, and internal departments to ensure accurate records and timely resolution of discrepancies. You will also support month-end and year-end closing by preparing schedules, journal entries, and account analyses, while assisting with audits and system-related matters. This role offers opportunities to improve workflows, strengthen controls, and contribute to ongoing process enhancements within the finance function. Main Duties and Responsibilities Perform daily reconciliation of POS sales data from all outlets across multiple sales and payment platforms. Investigate and resolve discrepancies by liaising with outlet managers and relevant internal teams. Manage and reconcile voucher reimbursements from various channels, including mall-issued vouchers, and other external partners. Track submissions, verify validity, follow up on pending claims, and ensure timely reimbursement. Prepare and issue invoices to customers and partners and monitor AR aging reports , follow up proactively on overdue balances, and work with outlets and external customers to ensure timely collection. Assist in month-end and year-end closing , including preparing journal entries for unreconciled items, accruals, and AR adjustments. Support internal and external audits by providing documentation, explanations, and reconciliations for AR balances and system transactions. Identify and implement process improvements , such as automating recurring reconciliation tasks, standardizing reporting templates, and enhancing system functionalities to reduce errors and improve efficiency. Compute and validate GTO figures for all outlets and partners, ensuring accuracy for reporting, billing, and contract compliance. Job Requirements Diploma/Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field. Minimum 3-5 years of Accounts Receivable experience, preferably in the retail/F&B sector. Proficient in MS Excel and familiar with accounting/ERP systems (Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central). Strong follow-up skills, with the ability to communicate clearly and effectively across all levels. Detail-oriented, analytical, and able to work independently in a fast-paced environment.

title: Director
best_module: ACC4761A – Seminars in Accounting: Internal Audit
description: A company director manages a company's affairs, sets its strategic direction, and is responsible for complying with corporate filings and record-keeping . Directors must act in good faith and in the company's best interests, avoiding conflicts of interest and exercising proper diligence. They have legal duties and can face civil or criminal penalties, including fines, imprisonment, or disqualification, for failing to fulfill these responsibilities. Responsibilities of a Company Director Manage company affairs :This includes making decisions about opening bank accounts, selling assets, and investing company funds. Strategic Direction :A director helps set the company's long-term goals and business direction. Record Keeping :Ensuring proper and up-to-date records of transactions and financial statements are kept. Compliance :Filing necessary corporate documents, tax returns, and other filings with regulatory bodies like the Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority (ACRA). Fiduciary Duties : Act in good faith : Conduct the company's affairs with honesty and integrity. Act in the company's best interests : Prioritize the company, its shareholders, and its stakeholders. Exercise due diligence : Apply reasonable care and skill in carrying out their duties. Avoid conflicts of interest : Disclose any personal interests that could conflict with the company's interests.

title: WTT Finance Director
description: ABOUT WORLD TABLE TENNIS World Table Tennis is where the sport’s fiercest battles meet bold ideas and creative minds. WTT is powered by a global crew of innovators, storytellers, and game-changers, who bring energy, expertise, and imagination to everything they do. We’re not your typical workplace; we’re the global engine behind a sport on the rise. So whether it’s staging iconic global events or redefining fan experiences, this is where bold ideas get built, and where every role has impact. At WTT, the arena simply hits different. FINANCE DIRECTOR To lead the corporate group’s finance function end-to-end, ensuring strong financial governance, reliable reporting, disciplined cashflow management, and commercial finance partnership to support sustainable growth. This role owns the “finance operating system”; standards, controls, risk posture, stakeholder reporting, and decision support while developing a high-performing lean team. RESPONSIBILITIES FINANCE LEADERSHIP & EXECUTIVE PARTNERING Act as a trusted advisor to CEO/senior leadership/Board/shareholders on group financial performance, governance, and strategic decisions. Translate financial data into clear options and recommendations (trade-offs, risks, scenario impacts). Build a finance culture that is commercially minded, control-conscious, and operationally pragmatic. FINANCIAL REPORTING GOVERNANCE & CLOSE OVERSIGHT Own the finance calendar, reporting cadence, and quality gates (e.g., close timeline, reconciliations standards, review checklist). Review and sign off management accounts, forecasts, and key reporting before distribution to CEO/Board/shareholders. Ensure accounting treatments are appropriate, consistent, and audit-ready (including revenue recognition implications relevant to events/rights-based revenue). TREASURY, CASH STRATEGY & FINANCIAL RISK CONTROL Own group-level cash strategy: runway planning, liquidity thresholds, and cash visibility. Maintain and review a rolling cash forecast discipline (e.g., weekly 13-week view where needed). Oversee banking relationships, payment controls, signatory governance, and (where relevant) FX exposure management. Partner with HR on payroll planning and execution oversight (timeliness, accuracy, approvals). FINANCIAL CONTROLS, GOVERNANCE & COMPLIANCE Design, implement, and enforce the group’s Delegation of Authority (DoA) and financial control framework (approval flows, spend controls, credit controls). Monitor compliance, investigate control breaches, and drive remediation — keeping the business moving without letting it get sloppy. Own finance policies/SOPs and ensure the team operates consistently across entities. FP&A LEADERSHIP (Budgeting, Forecasting, Performance insights) Lead annual budget cycle and reforecast cadence; set standards for assumptions, documentation, and sign-offs. Deliver insight-led performance commentary (variance drivers, scenario planning, sensitivities). Build KPI reporting aligned to the business model (event profitability, margin drivers, cash conversion, pipeline impact). COMMERCIAL FINANCE & DECISION SUPPORT Partner business leads on pricing/margins, contract payment terms, cost models, and profitability analysis across events/sponsorship/broadcast/ticketing. Ensure strategic and/or long-term growth projects (e.g. strategic partnerships, joint ventures, product launches and redevelopment, investments and market entries) have finance-reviewed business cases with measurable outcomes. Provide post-event financial performance views and lessons learned to improve future planning. AUDIT, TAX & SHAREHOLDER REPORTING LEADERSHIP Own auditor relationship, audit plan/timeline, and closure of audit findings. Ensure tax governance: timely filings, appropriate positions, and oversight of complex tax matters (GST/VAT, withholding tax, income tax as applicable). Partner shareholder finance teams on consolidated reporting requirements and governance expectations. TEAM LEADERSHIP, OPERATING MODEL & CAPABILITY BUILDING Lead, coach, and develop the finance team; set clear standards, delegation, and accountability. Ensure finance work is scalable: documented processes, cross-training, and sensible automation. Drive continuous improvement of tools and workflows (accounting, approvals, payments) while ensuring controls are robust and processes remain efficient and user-friendly. REQUIREMENTS Essentials: 10+ years relevant finance experience. Professional qualification: CA / CPA / ACCA (or equivalent). Proven people management experience (leading and developing finance staff & building operating rhythm and standards). Strong competency across reporting, controls, FP&A, audit, and tax governance. High proficiency in MS365 especially Excel and comfort working with modern finance tools. Desirables: Experience in startups/high-growth companies (e.g., scaling finance operations; exposure to Series funding rounds). Experience working with diverse stakeholder groups across cultures and seniority (including shareholders and auditors). Industry exposure to sport, entertainment, media rights-based or event-led businesses.Familiarity with finance and payments systems such as Xero, Syft, ApprovalMax, Zoho, Airwallex (or similar). HOW TO APPLY Please proceed to the World Table Tennis page - careers.worldtabletennis.com Due to the high number of candidate applications expected, only those who are shortlisted will be contacted.

Working knowledge of sanctions screening and trade finance enhanced due diligence is preferred. Experience in investigation, case documentation, and STR preparation. Exposure to transaction monitoring threshold or parameter tuning is an advantage. Strong analytical and investigative skills with excellent attention to detail. Good written communication skills, particularly in report writing. Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills.

description: GLOBAL COMPLIANCE Our division prevents, detects and mitigates compliance, regulatory and reputational risk across the firm and helps to strengthen the firm’s culture of compliance. Compliance accomplishes these through the firm’s enterprise-wide compliance risk management program. As an independent control function and part of the firm’s second line of defence, Compliance assesses the firm’s compliance, regulatory and reputational risk; monitors for compliance with new or amended laws, rules and regulations; designs and implements controls, policies, procedures and training; conducts independent testing; investigates, surveils and monitors for compliance risks and breaches; and leads the firm’s responses to regulatory examinations, audits and inquiries. You'll be part of a team with members from a wide range of academic and professional backgrounds, such as law, accounting, sales, and trading. We look for those who possess sound judgment, curiosity, and are able to adapt to a changing regulatory landscape. RESPONSIBILITIES AND QUALIFICATIONS Business Supported: Global Investment Research Division (GIR). From macroeconomic forecasts to individual stock analysis, GIR develops tools and insights to help shape investment strategies for clients and the firm. GIR analysts/associates work on client-focused research in the equity, fixed-income, currency, and commodities markets, mining big data that enters markets around the world each day to identify game-changing insights. HOW YOU WILL FULFILL YOUR POTENTIAL Initiative, creativity and drive, together with the ability to work closely and comfortably with research analysts, are essential attributes of all group members. Because the role requires substantive interaction with research analysts and advising on rules and laws impacting research across jurisdictions in Asia Pacific (including Greater China), candidates with legal, regulatory, compliance or other investigatory experience, who can function in a compliance or legal arena, are being sought. Work with global team members located in New York, Singapore, Shanghai, Tokyo, Sydney, Bengaluru, Dallas and London. Advisory and Compliance Oversight. Providing advice to the GIR business, including senior management, on regulatory and reputational related matters, and acting as a resource on a daily basis for compliance and policy issues. Policies and Procedures. Ensuring that GIR has appropriate policies and procedures in place. Assessing, designing and driving completion of necessary upgrades, updates and new policies. Working closely with Legal and GIR to fulfill requirements. Regulatory Matters. Helping to manage GIR’s responses to requests for information from regulators, keeping apprised of latest regulatory developments as it relates to GIR. Training. Designing, executing and delivering training on policies and procedures. Controls/Surveillance/Testing. Creating controls, rolling out surveillance and monitoring of policies and procedures. SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE WE ARE LOOKING FOR PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Legal/Regulatory background Fluency in English and Chinese (reading, speaking and writing) as the role involves dealing with jurisdictions across Asia Pacific, including Greater China, products and regulations. Strong oral and written communication skills; ability to communicate confidently and effectively with a diverse audience Quick learner who can adapt well to a fluid regulatory and business environment Ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously, and to work effectively in a team Organized, detail-oriented, enthusiastic and professional ABOUT GOLDMAN SACHS At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world. Reporting to the Regional Head of Financial Crimes Compliance, the Regional Head of Investigations (RHoI) will oversee and govern the GFCC operations across a number of markets within the Region, including operational forecasting, planning, resourcing, training, quality controls and other enterprise and market-level requirements.  In close coordination with Market Compliance Officers (MCOs), the RHOI will assess relevant enterprise and local risks and regulatory requirements related to AML/CTF, Sanctions, and ABC casework in relevant markets and ensure compliance with American Express FCRM policy and investigative standards.  The RHOI will monitor market compliance with the American Express FCRM policy and related standards, oversee operations, and coordinate with and support MCOs and market teams, ensuring that investigations and SAR reporting meet local regulatory requirements and enterprise standards. The position will supervise teams of investigators in markets across the region. Responsibilities Provide strategic and operational leadership for financial crimes compliance activities across APAC, ensuring a globally consistent, risk-based approach to FCRM that meets compliance requirements related to investigations, SAR filing activities and related FCRM activities. Drive the execution of GFCC standards and initiatives across APAC, tailoring to market-specific nuances where necessary. Oversee operational forecasting, planning and resourcing requirements for APACs’ various market-level teams. Oversee the investigation of complex financial crimes cases, including money laundering, fraud, terrorist financing, sanctions violations, and other suspicious activity. Ensure effective escalation and timely resolution. Ensure the quality, accuracy, and timeliness of suspicious activity and transaction reporting and sanctions/PEP/Risk screening processes in line with local regulations and GFCC policy and standards. Provide guidance to local teams on documentation standards and regulatory expectations. Closely collaborate with other Market/Regional Heads of Investigations and SAR Reporting across the Region as well as International Markets for harmonization and best practice sharing. Monitor and assess emerging risks and regulatory changes in close coordination with the Regional FCC regulatory team within the region, identifying trends and developing strategies to include in investigative procedures and training. Enable effective ongoing training and deep awareness of the evolving financial crime environment for all the team members. Manage, mentor, and develop a high-performing team responsible for investigations work. Support the management of supervisory and regulatory examinations and audits. Ensure adherence to legal and regulatory standards as well as internal quality requirements for suspicious activity report filings. Collect, analyse and report on screening, investigations and SAR metrics for relevant legal entities at a minimum. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s degree required. 8+ years of experience in financial crimes compliance, anti-money laundering (AML), in particular experience in investigations within the financial services, with significant exposure to the APAC region. Deep understanding of financial crimes regulatory requirements and trends across the APAC region. Strong knowledge of global AML, CTF, and sanctions regulatory frameworks, with expertise in SAR/STR filings, investigations, and regulatory engagement. Experience managing and leading regional or multi-jurisdictional investigations programs in a global or regional financial institution. Proven ability to work effectively across diverse markets, cultures, and regulatory environments. Strong communication, stakeholder management, and leadership skills. Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills with a risk-based mindset. CAMS and/or ICA Certifications a plus. Job Description: About NUS Business School Founded in the same year that Singapore gained independence, NUS Business School stands today among the world’s leading business schools. It is distinctive for offering the best of global business knowledge with deep Asian insights, preparing students to lead Asian businesses to international success and to help global businesses succeed in Asia. For more than 50 years, NUS Business School has offered a rigorous, relevant and rewarding business education to outstanding students from across the world. That would not have been possible without a group of dedicated professionals who are passionate about building a positive environment to grow and groom future business leaders. And we are looking to add to our BIZ family! To learn more about the NUS Business School, please visit https://bschool.nus.edu.sg/ Job Description As part of the Student Life and Experiential Learning team, the Manager/Assistant Senior Manager will manage the Experiential Learning components of the NUS BBA, with primary responsibility for the Field Service Projects (FSP), a credit-bearing graduation requirement that integrates academic learning with real-world business problem-solving. This role provides end-to-end leadership of the FSP ecosystem, working closely with faculty, industry partners, students, and internal stakeholders to ensure academic rigor, operational excellence, and a high-quality student experience. At the same time, this role will also work closely with the Team Lead to oversee that Business Internships (BI), a key element of the Work Experience Milestone for NUS BBA students. Programme Ownership and Strategy Provide overall stewardship of Field Service Projects (FSP), including special series FSP with distinct objectives, as a core graduation requirement of the NUS BBA. Review and enhance FSP policies, frameworks, and processes to ensure alignment with programme learning outcomes and evolving industry expectations. Contribute to broader work experience and experiential learning strategy within the undergraduate curriculum. Industry and Faculty Engagement Develop, manage, and sustain relationships with industry partners to source meaningful, high-impact FSP projects. Work closely with faculty advisors to support project scoping, academic supervision, assessment alignment, and quality assurance. Serve as the key liaison between external partners, faculty, and the School. Student Experience and Delivery Oversee student lifecycle for FSPs, including project matching, briefings, timelines, issue management, and completion. Ensure consistent and transparent communication to students on expectations, requirements, and milestones. Manage student-related escalations and provide sound judgment on complex or exceptional cases. Operations and Governance Lead and execute end-to-end operational planning and execution of FSP cycles, including schedules, project approvals, grading liaison, and graduation clearance. Ensure compliance with University and School policies, assessment standards, and audit requirements. Analyse programme data and feedback to inform continuous improvement. Stakeholder and Team Collaboration Work closely with colleagues across the Undergraduate Studies Office, Career Services (BIZCareers), faculty teams, and central units. Where applicable, supervise or guide junior staff supporting experiential learning administration and delivery. Prepare management updates, reports, and recommendations for senior leadership. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree required; postgraduate qualification is an advantage. At least 5–8 years of relevant experience in higher education administration, programme management, or experiential learning initiatives. Experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder programmes is highly desirable. Strong programme management and organisational skills, with attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple timelines. Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, including confidence working with faculty and industry partners. Sound judgment, maturity, and discretion in handling student and academic matters. Comfortable working in a structured yet evolving academic environment. Data-informed, improvement-oriented mindset. (Appointment job grade will commensurate with the selected candidate's experience)

title: Deputy General Manager, Regional Compliance (ASEAN)
topic_label: financial crimes compliance, financial crime compliance, anti money laundering

description: Overview We are seeking a senior compliance leader to design and enforce a unified governance framework across our ASEAN subsidiaries. Reporting to the Regional Head of Legal & Compliance, you will act as a strategic partner to the business, ensuring that our cross-border network expansion (IPLC, IP Transit, SD-WAN) and multi-country operations align strictly with the diverse legal, tax, and telecom licensing requirements of the region. Key Responsibilities: Regional Regulatory Strategy: Oversee the regulatory roadmap for entering and operating in ASEAN markets (including Indonesia, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand, and Philippines). Manage the acquisition and maintenance of necessary telecom licenses (e.g., NFP/NSP/NTC) and serve as the primary escalation point for regional regulators. Cross-Border Operational Governance: Harmonize "General Affairs" and HR compliance across subsidiaries. Ensure local offices adhere to their respective labor laws (e.g., Employment Act, Omnibus Law) regarding hiring, expatriate visas, and statutory benefits. Data Privacy & Sovereignty: Lead the regional data privacy strategy. Navigate complex data sovereignty laws to ensure the compliant transfer of customer data and internal records between ASEAN entities and Regional HQ. Commercial Risk Management: Sit on the Joint Governance committee to review complex multi-country connectivity deals. Audit vendor and partner contracts to ensure "back-to-back" liability protection and adherence to anti-corruption (ABC) standards. Internal Audit & Control: Conduct regular compliance audits of local ASEAN offices, focusing on procurement integrity, safety (WSH/EHS) standards, and financial reporting. Stakeholder Management: Translate regional strategy into local compliance roadmaps and act as the bridge between local ASEAN teams and the Group Headquarters. Job RequirementsExperience & Qualifications: Bachelor’s Degree in Law, International Business, or a related discipline. A Master’s degree or professional compliance certification is an advantage. Minimum 12 years of experience in Compliance, Legal, or Regional Operations, with at least 5 years in a leadership role within the Telecommunications or ICT industry. Proven track record of managing compliance or operations across multiple ASEAN jurisdictions (experience with Indonesia, Vietnam, or Malaysia is highly valued). Technical Skills & Knowledge: Strong understanding of cross-border telecom regulations (IPLC, VPN, Internet) and the licensing frameworks within Southeast Asia. Familiarity with regional data privacy laws (PDPA, GDPR principles) and data sovereignty requirements. Knowledge of regional labor regulations and "General Affairs" management. Other Requirements: Business-level proficiency in English and Mandarin is required. This role necessitates frequent communication with Product and Engineering teams at our Regional Headquarters in China, as well as the review of technical contracts and legal policies in Mandarin. Willingness to travel within the ASEAN region (approx. 30-40%) as business needs require. Legally eligible to work in Singapore.
